headline: Trump suggests Iran war nearing end
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. stock markets surged Monday afternoon following comments from President Trump suggesting the conflict with Iran could conclude soon, while oil prices declined from recent highs.

raw.headline: Trump suggests Iran war nearing end
raw.summary: Investing.com -- U.S. stock markets surged Monday afternoon following comments from President Trump suggesting the conflict with Iran could conclude soon, while oil prices declined from recent highs.

headline: Airline and cruise stocks plunge as oil prices surge on Iran conflict
summary: Investing.com -- United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) shares fell 6% on Monday, while American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) dropped 5%, Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) declined 4.5%, and Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) slipped 3.8% as companies exposed to high oil prices sold off following a surge in crude prices.

headline: Take the Zacks Approach to Beat the Markets: Starbucks, Amgen, Allogene in Focus
summary: ALLO, SBUX and AMGN highlight how Zacks strategies and portfolio picks are delivering strong gains despite recent market volatility.

headline: Oil prices could correct down just as hard as they spiked, Piper warns
summary: Investing.com -- A dramatic surge in crude has not delivered the usual boost to U.S. oilfield services stocks, and the risk now is that prices could fall just as sharply, according to Piper Sandler analyst Derek Podhaizer in a note Monday.

raw.headline: Oil prices could correct down just as hard as they spiked, Piper warns

headline: Can the Fed look through an oil shock?
summary: Investing.com -- The latest U.S. data offers a clearer picture of the economy, but rising oil prices now pose the biggest risk to the inflation outlook, Barclays analyst Jonathan Miller acknowledged in a note to clients on Monday.

raw.headline: Can the Fed look through an oil shock?

headline: JPM’s Matejka warns ’things might need to get worse before they can get better’
summary: Investing.com -- JPMorgan’s Mislav Matejka warned in a note Monday that equity markets may face more short-term weakness before conditions stabilise and that “things might need to get worse before they can get better.”
